document.title: Software Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for SDK-MCW software used with the ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation, verification, traceability, hazard analysis, and regulatory design-control evidence for the detector software and firmware.
keyPoints: SDK-MCW performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing for the detector.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to SDK-MCW software.
Software and firmware modules include common functions, image acquisition, calibration, pre-processing, firmware update, xSystem, FPGA App, System task, Interface, cyber security, and error control.
Module verification and system validation tests are recorded as passing.

evidence.claim: The document is File No. A-TCF-5-046, Rev. No. 0, dated June/23/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-TCF-5-046 Rev. No 0 Date June/23/2020

evidence.claim: The report title identifies the product as ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: SDK-MCW performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: SDK-MCW is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the SDK-MCW software has a Moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the SDK-MCW software. Level of Concern : Moderate

evidence.claim: The level-of-concern section references FDA guidance for software in medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The development process states it fulfils 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The software architecture includes five independent modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Software architecture consists of five independent modules. Acquisition modules provide the ability to correct and process raw images obtained from detectors.

evidence.claim: The software function modules include common functions, image acquisition, calibration, pre-processing, and firmware update.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions module Image acquisition module Calibration module Pre-Processing module Firmware update module

evidence.claim: The firmware function modules include xSystem, FPGA App, System task, and Interface.
evidence.evidenceText: xSystem Module FPGA App Module System task Module Interface Module

evidence.claim: Cyber security requirements include Wi-Fi login password control and authentication/encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-C-1 Wi-Fi Login Password Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. SRS-C-2 Authentication and encryption Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Error control requirements include reconnection recovery and calibration image processing for image processing errors.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-E-1 Connect Error It should support recovery mode to enable reconnection in case of disconnection between the detector and SDK software. SRS-E-2 Image Processing Error When noise or line is appeared during the pre-processing the image received from the detector, it should provide a calibration image for normal process.

evidence.claim: Module verification was certified as properly accomplished.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the code review, we hereby certify that the module verification of Software is properly accomplished.

evidence.claim: System testing verified all configured module functions as adequate, with no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The software revision history lists SDK-MCW version 1.0.0.0 dated June/23/2020 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Date Name Description 1.0.0.0 June/23/2020 SDK-MCW Initial release, Dual polynomial fitting calibration Only for proprietary applications.

evidence.claim: The firmware revision history lists FIRMWARE version 1.0.0.0 dated June/23/2020 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Date Name Description 1.0.0.0 June/23/2020 FIRMWARE Initial release

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies section lists none.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2. Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, while the report text cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
Approval date conflict appears in the document: page 1 shows Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu June/30/2010, while page 6 shows Approved by Hur Nam Kyu Date: 25 June 2020.
Product model metadata is partly external to the report text: productSignal indicates HAD/A/F 1417-1717, while the report title states ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
OCR/text quality includes typographical issues such as FRIMWARE, Wires network, Pwr buttin, and HA-005.
